reasoning: For the short-term horizon (Sep 07), there is a fair chance (~35%) that regular weekly volatility creates a slight bump up in total assets compared to the late-August baseline data point, despite the overarching downward trend of Quantitative Tightening (QT). By Sep 30, however, a full month of QT (which continues to drain billions per month) almost guarantees a net lower figure, making an increase highly unlikely (12%). For the longer horizons (Nov 25 and Feb 26), uncertainty naturally rises; market consensus currently projects QT concluding somewhere in late 2025 or early 2026. This, compounded with the increasing base rate of potential macro interventions, emergency reserve injections, or typical organic expansion of the balance sheet post-QT, means the probabilities for Nov and Feb increasingly drift back toward 0.5 (18% and 25%, respectively).
